basics.name: Muhammad Ammar
basics.label: AI + Knowledge Graphs Engineer @ Siemens | MSc Electromobility-ACES @ FAU
basics.summary: Machine Learning engineer working on LLM pipelines, knowledge graphs, ontology-driven graph-based analytics, and applied AI. Pursuing an MSc in Electromobility-ACES at Friedrich-Alexander-Universitat Erlangen-Nurnberg while researching GenAI-enabled software ecosystem health monitoring solutions at Siemens.

work.name: Siemens AG + FAPS FAU
work.summary: Beyond the Agent: A Workflow-Level Review of Agentic AI Systems.
highlights: Research objective: How are agentic AI systems structured, controlled, and evaluated as end-to-end workflows, and where do current approaches lack system-level rigor?

work.name: Siemens AG
work.summary: Research on software ecosystem health monitoring, ontology-driven ETL, and LLM-based knowledge graph analytics.
highlights: Conducted a systematic literature review of software ecosystem health analysis, surveying 61 research articles and 416 health metrics to build a unified metric ontology for ecosystem health monitoring.
Designed ontology-driven ETL pipelines that transform research metrics, KPIs, and platform ecosystem data into structured Neo4j knowledge graphs.
Developed an LLM-based Text-to-Cypher pipeline that maps natural-language prompts to knowledge graph queries for software ecosystem health analytics.
Modeled software ecosystem health as a metric knowledge graph, connecting indicators, KPI trees, and graph algorithms for real-time analysis and visualization.
Developed an LLM-powered Prompt-to-Visualization pipeline to display the most relevant dashboard for a user query, lowering TTI from 2 minutes to 5 seconds.
Submitted 6 invention disclosures and authored 4 research papers.

work.name: FAPS FAU
work.summary: Architectural Paradigms of Multi-Agent Systems and Agentic AI in Intelligent Manufacturing Systems, A Systematic Literature Review.
highlights: Research objective: How have agent architectures evolved in manufacturing systems, and what architectural design principles emerge from this evolution that are relevant for manufacturing control?

work.name: NeuroOceans AI
work.summary: Built and evaluated audiovisual deepfake detection models.
highlights: Finetuned multiple audiovisual deepfake detection models and evaluated them on performance metrics.
Led the entire pipeline development, from dataset collection and preprocessing to model training and evaluation.

work.name: Learners.ai
work.summary: Built GPT-powered workflow automations and chatbots using LangChain and Pinecone.
highlights: Used LangChain and Pinecone to create a GPT-powered RAG chatbot that takes a sales call transcript and generates a sales proposal.
Developed a copywriter chatbot using LangChain Agents, tool calling, and a curated knowledge base.
Conducted Data Import Analysis for data transfer across CRMs, using Excel, SQL, and Python.
Created ad campaign effectiveness dashboards in HubSpot and Databox.

work.name: Robotics and Intelligent Systems Engineering (RISE) Laboratory
work.summary: Senior year research project focused on mobile robotics.
highlights: Engaged in a senior year research project, providing bi-weekly presentations to a supervisory panel.
Developed a control algorithm for a mobile robot using Arduino, ultrasonic sensors, IR sensors, and encoders.

Successfully secured funding through IGNITE National Grassroots Research Initiative 2022 and obtained funding for AHFE Conference registration fees under the Pakistan and European Unions Horizon 2020 ENHANCE project.

education.area: AI & Robotics in Extended Reality Seasonal School

education.institution: National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST)

publications.name: Prompt-to-Metric: LLMs and Graph Algorithms for Platform Ecosystem Health Monitoring.

publications.name: Schema Injection Strategies for LLM-Based Text-to-Cypher Query Translation: An Empirical Study.

publications.name: A Chain-Driven Live Roller Mechanism for Loading and Unloading Packages on Autonomous Mobile Robots in Warehouses.
publications.publisher: Applied Human Factors and Ergonomics (AHFE) Conference
publications.summary: Conference paper on an autonomous mobile robot mechanism for warehouse loading and unloading.

projects.name: Autonomous Mobile Robot
projects.summary: Designed and developed an autonomous mobile robot for warehouse operations, including obstacle avoidance and an attachable live-roller mechanism for automated loading and unloading.

projects.name: Image2Audio app
projects.summary: Users upload an image, the app generates a caption for the image, creates a short story based on the caption, and then converts the story into audio format.

projects.name: Q&A Chatbot on private data
projects.summary: Blog post on Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) using ChromaDB, GPT wrapped around LangChain, and private data.

projects.name: Scene Classification
projects.summary: Finetuned ViT pre-trained on ImageNet for Kaggle Intel Classification Challenge.

projects.name: Podcast Summarizer
projects.summary: Converts podcast audio to text using Whisper, uses GPT-3.5 to generate a summary, and uses the Wikipedia API to display additional information.

projects.name: Transfer Learning with PyTorch
projects.summary: Scene classification by fine-tuning pre-trained Vision Transformers (ViT) guide.
